description: Designed and optimized CAD models for the Open Parametric Hand to improve modular prosthetic architectures. Developed parametric design strategies for robotic hand systems and executed rapid prototyping cycles using 3D printing to ensure scalable, cost-effective development.

Led full system development for a multi-channel infusion system used in an ex vivo cardiac revival platform. Designed a programmable architecture featuring Bluetooth control, PCB routing, electromechanical integration, and micro metal-gear motors with encoder-based calibration.

Designed the actuation-control subsystem for an automated blood smear device for sickle-cell screening, achieving 100 mm/s linear motion. Conducted engineering analysis on the Helios 500 intraoral scanner to propose optical and board-level circuitry improvements.

company: Dr. A. P. J. Abdul Kalam Technical University
description: Pursuing a Bachelor of Technology with a CGPA of 7.94/10. Coursework focuses on Embedded System Design, FPGA Architecture, Control Systems, Real-Time Systems, and Analog/Digital Electronics.
